theory.canonical_statement: $ε sin[nωrotationt])$ (123) Formal Technical Dissertation ID 534 formalizes the radial modulation of the containment shell’s information hologram[cite: 22]. It ensures that the stellar positions maintain an optimal packing density relative to the Alfvén velocity $vA$ of the vortex[cite: 7, 22]. This wave-vector coupling aligns mechanical displacement with the acoustic wave potentials $Φacoustic[cite:$ 18, 22]. [ID 535] QUANTUM TELEPORTATION MANIFOLD (Ω) Canonical Statutory Formula $Ω[T$ 2 × $T 2, π,Ψ] =⇒$ Error-Free State Transfer (124) Formal Technical Dissertation ID 535 establishes the double-torus $T$ 2 × $T$ 2 geometry used for fault-tolerant state transfer. This manifold enables bidirectional information transfer through the 2D collapsed space of the TZP interface, bypassing conventional 3D spatial constraints. The teleportation protocol achieves a capacity of $C ≈$ 18 qubits per use, utilizing the $π-modulated$ sphere packing as an entangled resource state.
theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of T 2, π,Ψ] is an algebraic definition, written T 2, π,Ψ] =⇒. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
